commit.message:
feat(language): add fast-mode support for Lua (#176)

* feat(trace): add fast-mode Lua support

* fix(trace): dedupe lua refs and skip declaration noise

Deduplicate overlapping regex matches in fast-mode Lua trace output so method-style calls do not produce repeated logical refs.

Also skip call-like matches that occur inside Lua function declarations, which removes self-noise such as function M.foo(x) being interpreted as a call site.

While touching the Lua method patterns, add support for conservative static bracket-key definitions like obj["foo"] = function(...) so symbol extraction stays aligned with bracket-key call support.

Parenthesis-free Lua calls remain intentionally unsupported in fast mode because supporting them cleanly would require adding several Lua-only pattern types to LanguagePatterns for relatively little value.

commit.message:
feat(cli): add shell completion command with dynamic completions (#175)

* feat(cli): add shell completion command with dynamic completions

Add `grepai completion [zsh|bash|fish|powershell]` command that generates
shell completion scripts with intelligent completions for flag values
and positional arguments.

Static completions:
- init --provider/--backend
- workspace create --provider/--backend
- trace callers/callees/graph --mode

Dynamic completions:
- --workspace flag on search, watch, mcp-serve, trace commands
- --project flag on search (depends on --workspace value)
- workspace show/delete/status args (workspace names)
- workspace add args (workspace name + directory)
- workspace remove args (workspace name + project names)

* fix(cli): use cmd.OutOrStdout() to avoid data race in completion tests

Redirect completion output through Cobra's OutOrStdout() instead of
os.Stdout directly. Tests now use rootCmd.SetOut() to capture output
without mutating the global os.Stdout, fixing the data race detected
on Windows CI with -race -coverprofile.

* docs: add shell completion to CHANGELOG and README

---------

Co-authored-by: Gauthier Painteaux <gauthier.painteaux@koul.io>

commit.message:
refactor(trace): deduplicate workspace helpers, eliminate double scan, add error logging (#164)

* refactor(trace): address PR #121 review feedback

Extract shared LoadWorkspaceSymbolStores and CloseSymbolStores
into trace/workspace.go, eliminating ~50 lines of duplication
between cli/trace.go and mcp/server.go.

Add ScannedFiles field to IndexStats so runInitialScan reuses
the file list from IndexAllWithBatchProgress instead of calling
scanner.ScanMetadata() a second time.

Replace ~21 silent error discards (_, _ :=) on symbol store
and config operations with log.Printf warnings in both
cli/trace.go and mcp/server.go.

* test(trace): add workspace symbol store helper tests

Covers LoadWorkspaceSymbolStores with missing config, unknown
workspace, unknown project, multi-project loading, and single
project filtering. Also tests CloseSymbolStores.

* style: fix whitespace formatting from pre-commit checks

Remove trailing whitespace and normalize alignment
detected by gofmt and whitespace hooks.

commit.message:
docs: add agent integration step to quickstart (#167)

* docs: add agent integration step to quickstart

The quickstart currently ends at "search your code" and "check status",
but most grepai users are using it with an AI coding agent. Without the
MCP or subagent setup step, the index gets built but the agent has no
way to access it.

Add step 5 pointing users to the MCP and subagent docs, and remove the
now-redundant agent-setup link from Next Steps (the new step covers it
with better context).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify MCP vs agent-setup vs subagent in step 5

- Make clear that MCP is the recommended integration path
- Explain that agent-setup is a CLI-based alternative for agents
  without MCP support, and is not needed when using MCP
- Keep subagent mention light — one line pointing to its page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(indexer): add .grepaiignore support with negation patterns (#163)

Add support for .grepaiignore files that allow overriding .gitignore
rules for grepai indexing. This enables users with nested repos or
specific directory structures to force-include paths that .gitignore
would normally exclude.

Key features:
- .grepaiignore files parsed alongside .gitignore at any directory level
- Negation patterns (!) re-include files excluded by .gitignore
- Directory-level precedence: deeper .gitignore wins over shallower
  .grepaiignore negations
- Same-level .grepaiignore always overrides same-level .gitignore
- ShouldSkipDir prevents premature directory pruning when negations exist
- Scanner and watcher updated to use ShouldSkipDir for correct traversal

Closes #107

Co-authored-by: Gauthier Painteaux <gauthier.painteaux@koul.io>

commit.message:
refactor(rpg): rename RPGIndexer to RPGEncoder and extend multi-feature model (#149)

* refactor(rpg): rename RPGIndexer to RPGEncoder and extend multi-feature model

Rename RPGIndexer/RPGIndexerConfig to RPGEncoder/RPGEncoderConfig to
align with RPG-Encoder paper terminology. Add Node.Features field for
atomic semantic features, version-gated GOB store with ErrRPGIndexOutdated,
platform flock for concurrent access, Summarizer for hierarchy nodes,
and file-level fallback in search RPG enrichment. Improve MCP error
handling for outdated RPG indices.

Includes trace package changes: docstring extraction for Go symbols
and flock-based GOBSymbolStore concurrent access protection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(rpg): consolidate RPG documentation into single technical report

* refactor: extract shared fileutil package from duplicated flock implementations

Consolidate 6 duplicated flock files (rpg/, store/, trace/) into a single
internal/fileutil package with platform-specific implementations.

Changes:
- Add internal/fileutil/ with FlockExclusive, FlockShared, Funlock (unix/windows)
- Add EnsureParentDir and ReplaceFileAtomically shared helpers
- Add nonBlocking parameter to flock functions for graceful degradation
- Fix lock ordering: flock acquired BEFORE mu.Lock() to prevent stalls
- Add log.Printf warnings at all flock fallback points in rpg/store_gob.go
- Migrate rpg/store_gob.go, store/gob.go, trace/store.go to use fileutil
- Delete 6 duplicated flock files (-236 lines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(rpg): add RemoveEdgesBetweenOfType for precise edge cleanup

Add a type-filtered variant of RemoveEdgesBetween that removes only edges
of a specific EdgeType between two nodes, preserving other edge types.

This enables the evolver to cleanly reassign file-to-hierarchy edges
(EdgeFeatureParent) without destroying co-occurrence or containment edges
between the same node pair.

- Add Graph.RemoveEdgesBetweenOfType(from, to, edgeType) method
- Maintain forward/reverse adjacency indexes during removal
- Add comprehensive test covering selective removal behavior
- Add TODO comments for AddNode optimization and ParseNodeID utility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(rpg): propagate context.Context, add thread safety and deterministic RNG

Breaking change: FeatureExtractor interface now requires context.Context
as first parameter on ExtractFeature and ExtractAtomicFeatures methods.

Context propagation:
- Thread ctx through FeatureExtractor interface, all implementations,
  and all callers (evolver, indexer, hierarchy)
- Remove stored ctx field from LLMExtractor (accept per-call instead)
- Eliminate all context.Background() calls from evolver

Thread safety:
- Upgrade RPGEncoder.mu from sync.Mutex to sync.RWMutex
- Add Stats() method with RLock for concurrent-safe graph statistics
- Add concurrent Stats access test with race detector
- Update cli/watch.go to use encoder.Stats() instead of graph.Stats()
- Document GetGraph() concurrency contract

Deterministic RNG:
- Add Seed field to RPGEncoderConfig for reproducible shuffling
- Add rng *rand.Rand field to RPGEncoder (seeded at construction)
- Pass rng to capGroup instead of using global rand
- Add nolint:gosec annotation (not security-sensitive)

Other improvements:
- Add maxCoCallerCallees=50 cap to limit hub caller edge explosion
- Use RemoveEdgesBetweenOfType for precise edge cleanup in evolver
- Add io.LimitReader(64KB) on LLM response body reads
- Remove dead code (sanitizeLabel comment, WithContext method)
- Fix step numbering in BuildFull (4→3, 5→4, etc.)
- Replace fmt.Printf with log.Printf for operational warnings
- Add selectSubcategoryByFrequency and O(C+S) lookup map in hierarchy
- Add 15 test cases for parseAtomicFeatureResponse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): add query safety guards and summarization circuit breaker

Query safety:
- Add 1MB file size check in readNodeCodePreview before os.ReadFile
- Refactor getFeaturePath to getFeaturePathWithDepth with depth cap at 4
  to prevent unbounded recursion in malformed graphs
- Replace custom minInt helper with built-in min()
- Add empty NodeID validation in FetchNode

Summarization resilience:
- Add circuit breaker pattern to summarizeNodes: abort after 3
  consecutive LLM failures to prevent cascading timeouts
- Counter resets on success, scoped per node kind (subcategory/
  category/area) so transient failures don't block other phases
- Replace fmt.Printf with log.Printf for failure messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: format rpg indexer test for lint

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(release): bump version to 0.33.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(trace): add F# language support with Ionide tree-sitter grammar (#152)

* feat(trace): add F# language support with Ionide tree-sitter grammar

Add comprehensive F# support including:

Regex extractor (fast mode):
- Function patterns: let, let rec, let private, let inline bindings
- Method patterns: member, static member, abstract member, override
- Type patterns: discriminated unions, records, abbreviations
- Class patterns: types with constructors, modules
- Interface patterns: types matching I+uppercase naming convention
- F# block comment (* *) filtering in buildIgnoredMask
- Indentation-based function boundary detection

Tree-sitter extractor (precise mode, behind treesitter build tag):
- Vendor Ionide tree-sitter-fsharp grammar (v0.1.0, ABI 14)
- Symbol extraction from value_declaration, type_definition,
  member_defn, module_defn AST nodes
- Reference extraction from application_expression nodes
  (handles both juxtaposition and dotted calls)
- Interface detection via abstract-only member analysis

Config and documentation:
- Add .fs, .fsx, .fsi to default enabled languages
- Add .fs, .fsx, .fsi to watcher traced languages
- Update trace docs, watch guide, and homepage

Bug fix (pre-existing, all languages):
- Fix findContainingFunction() in regex extractor failing to match
  functions at file position 0 (zero-value comparison bug)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(trace): address PR review — deduplicate interface/type, nested comments, indexer pattern

- Types regex now excludes I+uppercase names to avoid extracting
  interfaces as both KindInterface and KindType
- MethodCall regex no longer matches bracket indexers (.[idx]),
  only parenthesized method calls
- Nested F# block comments (* outer (* inner *) still comment *)
  now tracked with depth counter instead of exiting on first *)
- Added test assertions for all three fixes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(daemon): detach background watcher from parent console on Windows

The background child process inherited the parent's console because
sysProcAttr() returned nil on Windows. When the parent exited after
writing the ready file, Windows sent CTRL_CLOSE_EVENT to the child
(mapped to SIGTERM by Go), killing the watcher immediately after
initial scan.

Set CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP | CREATE_NO_WINDOW when spawning the
child to fully detach from the parent's console, matching the Unix
behavior where Setpgid detaches from the parent's process group.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: worktree dedup and multi-project chunk storage (#142)

* fix: worktree dedup and multi-project chunk storage

Fix two issues that break content-hash dedup across git worktrees
sharing a PostgreSQL backend:

1. Files with documents but no chunks are permanently skipped.
   When a prior indexing run creates document records but fails to
   embed (e.g., missing API key), subsequent runs skip those files
   because the mod-time gate and hash check don't verify chunk
   existence. Fix: check len(doc.ChunkIDs) > 0 before skipping in
   IndexAllWithBatchProgress and NeedsReindex.

2. Chunk IDs collide across projects sharing the same database.
   Chunk IDs use relative paths (e.g., src/App.tsx_0). With the
   primary key on just (id), ON CONFLICT upserts from a second
   project overwrite the first project's chunks without updating
   project_id — the second project ends up with 0 chunks.
   Fix: migrate primary key to (project_id, id) and update the
   ON CONFLICT clause to match.

Tested with 3 projects sharing one postgres instance:
- 100% content-hash cache hit rate for identical code
- ~9s per worktree (vs ~5min without cache)
- Each project gets its own chunk rows

* fix: update tests to match new ChunkIDs-aware skip logic

The indexer now calls GetDocument before the lastIndexTime gate and
requires doc.ChunkIDs to be non-empty for both the time-based and
hash-based skip paths. This prevents silently skipping files whose
prior indexing run created a document but failed to embed chunks.

Update tests to seed documents with ChunkIDs where the test expects
a skip, and remove assertions that GetDocument should not be called
(it is now called by design before the time check).

fix(trace): improve callgraph quality and GOB store resilience (#137)

- Add buildIgnoredMask to skip false-positive references inside strings
  and comments during regex-based call extraction
- Add func to Go keyword list to filter anonymous function artifacts
- Improve GetCallGraph algorithm:
  - Prevent explosion through ambiguous/unknown intermediate symbols
  - Skip declaration self-edge artifacts (func name matching its own line)
  - Deduplicate edges globally across BFS traversal
  - Limit callers lookup to root symbol only (depth 0)
- Add ensureParentDir to GOB stores (trace, store) for auto-creating
  missing parent directories on Persist

- Preserve source file permissions in copy fallback instead of hardcoded 0755
- Add unit tests for safeRename (happy path + permissions preserved)

commit.message:
feat(rpg): add RPG semantic graph layer fully integrated into existing APIs (#110)

* feat(rpg): add RPG semantic graph layer integrated into existing APIs

Implement the RPG-Encoder paper concepts (arXiv 2602.02084) as a
transparent enhancement to existing search and index status tools,
rather than separate commands.

New rpg/ package provides:
- Graph model with 6 node kinds and 5 edge types
- Local heuristic feature extraction (verb-object from names)
- Optional LLM-based feature extraction
- 3-level hierarchy (area/category/subcategory)
- Incremental evolution with Jaccard drift detection
- Query engine (SearchNode, FetchNode, Explore BFS)
- GOB persistence, gated by cfg.RPG.Enabled

Integration points:
- grepai search: transparently enriches results with feature_path
  and symbol_name when RPG is enabled
- grepai_search MCP tool: same enrichment for AI agents
- grepai_index_status MCP tool: includes RPG stats when enabled
- grepai watch: builds and maintains RPG graph incrementally

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): address review findings for RPG integration

- [Critical] Fix BuildFull graph reconstruction: clear graph in-place
  instead of reassigning local variable, so store/hierarchy/evolver
  all operate on the same graph instance
- [High] Wire LLM extractor at runtime based on cfg.RPG.FeatureMode
  instead of always using NewLocalExtractor()
- [High] Fix LLMExtractor timeout bug: store resolved timeout back
  into config so context.WithTimeout uses same value as HTTP client
- [High] Fix feature_path duplication: hierarchy nodes already store
  full paths in Feature field, return directly instead of walking up
  and joining (which produced "cli/cli/watch/cli/watch/handle")
- [Medium] Route create events to HandleAdd instead of HandleModify
  so new files get proper file node creation and hierarchy linking
- [Medium] Populate symbols and call edges in BuildFull via new
  SymbolStore.GetSymbolsForFile and GetCallEdges interface methods
- [Medium] Allow drift_threshold=0 by changing applyDefaults check
  from <= 0 to < 0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): address second review round and CONTRIBUTING.md compliance

- Fix SA5011 lint: use t.Fatal on nil check to prevent nil deref
- Add LLM config validation: warn and fallback when endpoint/model empty
- Add EdgeMapsToChunk linking in incremental watch path
- Improve callee matching: prefer same-file symbol on name collision
- Add RPG feature entry to CHANGELOG.md
- Apply gofmt formatting fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): third review round - chunk dedup, LLM defaults, CHANGELOG accuracy

- Fix EdgeMapsToChunk accumulation: clean up existing chunk nodes before
  re-linking to prevent infinite edge/node growth on repeated file edits
- Add default LLM config: lfm2.5-thinking via Ollama as default RPG model
  (users only need rpg.enabled + feature_mode: llm)
- Fix CHANGELOG: remove non-existent MCP tool names, clarify RPG enhances
  existing grepai_search
- Add regression test: TestLinkChunksForFile_NoAccumulation verifies
  repeated calls don't accumulate edges

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): use t.TempDir() in test for Windows CI compatibility

Replace hardcoded "/dev/null" with filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "rpg.gob")
to avoid platform-dependent test failures on Windows CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): fix search enrichment overlap + remove double persist

- Fix asymmetric line overlap check in enrichWithRPG: use symmetric
  overlap (start1 <= end2 && start2 <= end1) matching indexer.go logic.
  Previously missed chunks starting before a symbol but overlapping it.
- Remove redundant Persist() calls from HandleFileEvent and
  LinkChunksForFile. The watch loop's 30-second ticker and shutdown
  handler already persist. Eliminates double GOB encode per file event.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): normalize EndLine=0 from regex extractor to fix RPG enrichment

The regex-based trace extractor (fast mode) never populates EndLine,
leaving it as 0. This caused all overlap checks to fail silently,
resulting in zero EdgeMapsToChunk/EdgeInvokes edges and empty
feature_path/symbol_name in search results.

Fix: normalizeEndLine() falls back to StartLine when EndLine is 0,
applied at all symbol node creation points (BuildFull, addSymbolNode,
HandleModify). Before: 2863 edges (0 invokes, 0 maps_to_chunk).
After: 4980 edges (880 invokes, 1228 maps_to_chunk).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(rpg): integrate RPG feature paths into trace callers/callees/graph

Enrich all trace command outputs with RPG semantic hierarchy paths:
- Add FeaturePath field to trace.Symbol struct
- CLI: enrichTraceWithRPG() loads RPG store and annotates all symbols
- MCP: enrichTraceSymbols() method on Server for all 3 trace handlers
- Human-readable display shows "Feature:" lines when available

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: enhance RPG indexer with hierarchy label enrichment, import wiring, improved invocation resolution, and semantic similarity edges based on feature overlap and co-caller affinity.

* docs(contributing): update Go version and project structure

- Update Go prerequisite from 1.22 to 1.24 to match go.mod
- Add missing directories to project structure: daemon, docs, mcp, rpg,
  trace, updater
- Update existing directory descriptions (embedder, store, search)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): use exclusive lock in GOBRPGStore.Persist to prevent race condition

RLock allows concurrent readers, but GOB encoding traverses the graph
which can race with concurrent writes. Switch to Lock/Unlock for safe
serialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mcp): add nil pointer guard and RPG enrichment failure logging

- Check both rpgSt and qe for nil before using query engine
- Log warnings when RPG enrichment is unavailable instead of silently skipping
- Return actual errors from tryLoadRPG for config/store load failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): propagate parent context in LLMExtractor

Add ctx field and WithContext() method so callers can pass their context
for proper cancellation propagation instead of using context.Background().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): deduplicate index entries in AddNode to prevent memory growth

When a node with the same ID is re-added, remove old byKind, byFile,
and byFeaturePath index entries before inserting new ones. Prevents
stale reference accumulation on repeated updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rpg): cap wireFeatureSimilarity groups and extract constants

- Add maxFeatureGroupSize (100) to skip oversized verb groups and
  mitigate O(n²) comparisons in wireFeatureSimilarity
- Extract magic numbers as named constants: minFeatureSimilarity,
  minCoCallerCoOccurrence, coCallerWeightNorm

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(config): add RPG configuration validation and default constants

- Add ValidateRPGConfig() with range checks for drift_threshold (0-1),
  max_traversal_depth (1-10), and enum check for feature_mode
- Extract RPG defaults as named constants (DefaultRPGDriftThreshold, etc.)
- Call validation in Load() when RPG is enabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: align Go version to 1.23 across go.mod, CI, and docs

- Set go.mod to go 1.23.0 (minimum required by mcp-go)
- Update CI workflow: lint, test, build all use Go 1.23
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md prerequisites to Go 1.23
- Downgrade deps requiring Go 1.24: x/sync, x/crypto, x/net, x/sys,
  x/text, grpc, genproto, qdrant/go-client

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(rpg): add configurable feature group strategy for wireFeatureSimilarity

Add "sample" (random sampling, default) and "split" (directory-based
partitioning) strategies for capping large verb groups. The "split"
strategy preserves locality by grouping symbols by directory before
pairwise comparison, with sampling fallback per subgroup if still
over cap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add RPG feature status to watch command output

* chore(fmt): apply gofmt to fix lint

* fix(trace): curb overbroad callgraph expansion

* feat: reduce trace graph noise and harden worktree discovery

* feat: ignore anonymous func artifacts in trace extraction

* fix(pr): restore upstream go and dependency baselines

* test(cli): align watch tests with merged handler signature

* fix(watch): improve realtime RPG updates and workspace runtime

* fix(rpg): serialize indexer graph mutations in watch path

* fix: GOB stores now automatically create missing parent directories when persisting data.

* fix: clean up PR review issues before merge

- Remove dead indexWorkspaceProject function (replaced by initializeWorkspaceRuntime)
- Fix DriftThreshold default not applying when YAML field omitted (< 0 → == 0)
- Remove hardcoded lfm2.5-thinking LLM model default (user must configure explicitly)
- Add intentional-duplication comments to ensureParentDir across store/trace/rpg
- Fix misleading comment on ValidateWatchConfig call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: revert CHANGELOG and CONTRIBUTING changes out of RPG branch scope

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: revert trace/store independent improvements out of RPG branch scope

Remove non-RPG changes that were mixed into this branch:
- trace/extractor.go: buildIgnoredMask (string/comment filtering)
- trace/patterns.go: func keyword addition
- trace/store.go: callgraph algorithm (shouldTraverse, isDeclarationSelfEdge)
- trace/store.go: ensureParentDir
- store/gob.go: ensureParentDir
- trace/store_graph_test.go: callgraph algorithm tests

Retain RPG-supporting additions in trace/store.go:
- GetSymbolsForFile (used by RPG indexer)
- GetCallEdges (used by RPG indexer)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(store): add GOB file locking for cross-process safety (#113)

Add flock-based file locking to GOBStore Load and Persist operations
for cross-process safety when multiple processes access the same index
file (e.g., watch daemon + search command running concurrently).

- Unix: uses flock(2) syscall (shared lock for read, exclusive for write)
- Windows: uses LockFileEx/UnlockFileEx Win32 API
- Backward compatible: falls back to unlocked access if lock fails

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(store): add content-addressed embedding deduplication (#112)

Add ContentHash field to Chunk (SHA256 of raw content, path-independent)
and EmbeddingCache interface for cross-worktree dedup. When a store
implements EmbeddingCache, the indexer checks for existing embeddings
by content hash before calling the embedder, avoiding redundant API
calls for identical content (file renames, git worktrees).

Implementations:
- GOB: linear scan over in-memory chunks
- Postgres: indexed content_hash column with partial index
- Qdrant: keyword field index on content_hash payload

commit.message:
fix(chunker): align chunk boundaries to valid UTF-8 rune starts (#96) (#116)

When a chunk boundary falls in the middle of a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence
(e.g. box-drawing ═, emojis 🚀, CJK characters), the resulting slice
produces invalid UTF-8 that PostgreSQL rejects with
"invalid byte sequence for encoding UTF8".

Add alignRuneBoundary() helper that adjusts byte offsets forward (1-3
bytes max) to the next valid rune start. Applied at the 4 critical
split points in Chunk() and ReChunk().

ASCII-only files are completely unaffected since all ASCII bytes are
valid rune starts.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(release): bump version to 0.25.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add progress reporting for sequential (Ollama) indexing (#94)

When using Ollama as the embedding provider, the indexer falls back to
sequential file-by-file embedding since Ollama doesn't implement the
BatchEmbedder interface. Previously, this sequential path had no progress
feedback, causing users to see the scan complete quickly but then appear
to hang during the slow embedding phase (which can take 30+ minutes for
large codebases).

This fix:
- Adds BatchProgressInfo callbacks to the sequential indexing loop
- Updates printBatchProgress() to show a visual progress bar matching
  the scan progress bar style

Fixes #87

fix: add progress reporting for sequential (Ollama) indexing (#94)

When using Ollama as the embedding provider, the indexer falls back to
sequential file-by-file embedding since Ollama doesn't implement the
BatchEmbedder interface. Previously, this sequential path had no progress
feedback, causing users to see the scan complete quickly but then appear
to hang during the slow embedding phase (which can take 30+ minutes for
large codebases).

This fix:
- Adds BatchProgressInfo callbacks to the sequential indexing loop
- Updates printBatchProgress() to show a visual progress bar matching
  the scan progress bar style

Fixes #87

Implement automatic chunk splitting when embedder context limit is exceeded.

- Add comprehensive tests for error detection and re-chunking logic

fix(config): resolve symlinks in FindProjectRoot (#85)

When grepai watch is executed from a symlinked directory, no files
were being indexed because os.Getwd() returns the symlink path without
resolving it.

This fix adds filepath.EvalSymlinks() to resolve the real path before
searching for the project root, ensuring proper file indexing from
symlinked directories.

feat(workspace): add multi-project workspace support (#75)

* feat(workspace): add multi-project workspace support

Implement workspace functionality for indexing and searching across
multiple projects using a shared vector store.

Features:
- New `grepai workspace` command with create/list/show/add/remove/delete
- Extended `watch` command with --workspace flag for multi-project indexing
- Extended `search` command with --workspace and --project flags
- MCP server support for workspace-scoped queries
- Background daemon support for workspace watchers
- Full documentation and blog post

Technical details:
- File paths stored as workspaceName/projectName/relativePath
- Prevents conflicts between workspaces with same project names
- Configuration priority: store/embedder from workspace, chunking/ignore from project
- GOB backend not supported for workspaces (requires shared store)

100% backward compatible - existing single-project workflows unchanged.

Closes #55

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

readme_html:
grepai

grep for the AI era

Search code by meaning, not just text.

Documentation · Installation · Quick Start

grepai is a privacy-first CLI for semantic code search. It uses vector embeddings to understand code meaning, enabling natural language queries that find relevant code—even when naming conventions vary.

Drastically reduces AI agent input tokens by providing relevant context instead of raw search results.

Features

  • Search by intent — Ask "authentication logic" and find handleUserSession
  • Trace call graphs — Know who calls a function before you change it
  • 100% local — Your code never leaves your machine
  • Always up-to-date — File watcher keeps the index fresh automatically
  • AI agent ready — Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf out of the box
  • MCP server — Your AI agent can call grepai directly as a tool

Installation

Homebrew (macOS):

brew install yoanbernabeu/tap/grepai

Linux/macOS:

curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yoanbernabeu/grepai/main/install.sh | sh

Windows (PowerShell):

irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yoanbernabeu/grepai/main/install.ps1 | iex

Requires an embedding provider — Ollama (default), LM Studio, or OpenAI.

Ollama (recommended):

ollama pull nomic-embed-text

Quick Start

grepai init                        # Initialize in your project
grepai watch                       # Start indexing daemon
grepai search "error handling"     # Search semantically
grepai trace callers "Login"       # Find who calls a function

Shell Completion

grepai supports autocompletion for commands, flags, and dynamic values (workspace names, project names, providers, backends).

Zsh (add to ~/.zshrc):

eval "$(grepai completion zsh)"

Oh-My-Zsh plugin:

mkdir -p ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-~/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/grepai
grepai completion zsh > ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-~/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/grepai/_grepai
# Then add "grepai" to plugins=(...) in ~/.zshrc

Bash:

# Linux
grepai completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/grepai

# macOS (requires bash-completion@2)
grepai completion bash > $(brew --prefix)/etc/bash_completion.d/grepai

Fish:

grepai completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/grepai.fish

PowerShell:

grepai completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

What developers say

"I just hit my limit and it took 13% of my max5 plan just to read my codebase. I am very, very excited about your new tool." — u/911pleasehold on r/ClaudeAI (280K+ views)

"It works great! Takes 5 minutes to install. Crazy!" — @LesSaleGeek on X

"The results are incredible!" — Kenny Nguyen on LinkedIn

Why grepai?

grep was built in 1973 for exact text matching. Modern codebases need semantic understanding.

grep / ripgrep grepai
Search Exact text / regex Semantic understanding
Query "func.*Login" "user authentication flow"
Finds Pattern matches Conceptually related code

Documentation

Full docs, guides, and blog:

  • Documentation — Configuration, AI agents, MCP setup
  • Blog — Benchmarks, tutorials, release notes

Contributing

See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.
